hill
Manor

Glouos

[?Selt]
South

house behind it, where he installed electricity &
hot water. He often sat there next to the fire
in a hall porter's chair. A rope was next to
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible on this page. The photo shows Snowshill Manor (Gloucestershire), a National Trust property. The caption is written to the right of the photo. One word before 'South Front' is unclear — possibly 'Selt' or a crossed-out word; could be a label direction like 'South Front'. The narrative text at bottom continues onto the right page. The volume covers 2003–2019 but no year is given on this page; context from right page ('23 May Sunday') does not uniquely anchor a year.

pages.full_text:
him & when he pulled it, it worked the
bellows in the next room. Bryony & I enjoyed
looking at the dolls' houses. Simon &
looked at the warping? machine, large,
6ft high, 6ft diametre, for # wrapping
silk thread around at the correct tension
for the weaver. Lots of spinning wheels.
   Wagons from different countries, ships,
clocks, walking frames for toddlers, one
for round a post (on a ship?) Crafts,
colours, useful, beautiful things. One of
the best places I've been.
   We had lunch in the carpark. They
drove back to London & we are going
back to Birmingham.
   __23 May Sunday__
   Traffic jams as we left [?Sham] at 1.30
to take Margaret & Helen out for a
Strawberry Tea. A lovely sunny
afternoon.
pages.notes: Page number 27 visible at top-right. '23 May Sunday' is underlined. The word 'warping?' is written with a query mark in the original text — transcribed as written. '# wrapping' — the '#' appears to be a correction mark or deletion in the original. The place '[?Sham]' is unclear — it could be an abbreviated local place name near Snowshill, possibly 'Snowshill' abbreviated, or another nearby village. 23 May falls on a Sunday in years such as 2004, 2010, 2021 — within the volume B range (2003–2019), 2004 and 2010 are candidates. 'Bryony', 'Simon', 'Margaret', and 'Helen' are named individuals. The diarist describes returning to Birmingham while others return to London.
